name: libwww-perl
title: libwww-perl
summary: simple and consistent interface to the world-wide web
description:
simple and consistent interface to the world-wide web
libwww-perl (also known as LWP) is a collection of Perl modules that provide
a simple and consistent programming interface (API) to the World-Wide Web.
The main focus of the library is to provide classes and functions that allow
you to write WWW clients. It also contains general purpose modules, as well
as a simple HTTP/1.1-compatible server implementation.

commands.name: lwp-download
commands.description: Fetch large files from the web

arguments.description: save the file in ASCII mode
arguments.raw: -a  save the file in ASCII mode

arguments.description: Fetch large files from the web
arguments.raw: -download  Fetch large files from the web

arguments.description: use HTTP headers to guess output filename
arguments.raw: -s  use HTTP headers to guess output filename

commands.name: lwp-dump
commands.description: See what headers and content is returned for a URL

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use lwp-download because simple and consistent interface to the world-wide web.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use lwp-download as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `lwp-download [-a] [-s] <url> [<local path>]` as documented upstream for lwp-download.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `lwp-download $KFIOSA_TARGET_URL` as documented upstream for lwp-download.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair lwp-download with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `lwp-download --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
